scope.placement_coverage: one explicit admitted named-label placement per profile

encoded: one common cyclic carrier order and its three closed Moser caps
six distinct named labels p,q,t1,t2,t3,u with q in the strict interior of the declared surplus cap
the exact supplied t2 row centered at p with support exactly {q,t1,t2,t3}
five supplied source-indexed rows as independent exact four-point filters with source membership and center distinct from source
one selected K4 row at every carrier center; the saved witnesses choose these rows exact, which is stronger than required
one source-indexed CriticalShellSystem row per carrier point, with source membership, center distinct from source, and support equal to the selected row at its blocker center
the CriticalShellSystem no-q-free consequence against every encoded selected row at each chosen blocker center
the current ATAIL inventory local row constraints, proven killed row types, Moser-pair Q3 cuts, cyclic two-common-point separation, and at-most-two-centers-per-point-pair bound
the card-12 cap profile, a surplus cap of size at least five, a distinct second cap of size at least five, and the cap-size consequence of no-M44
Q-C negation for encoded rows: no pair of dangerous-row points outside a cap containing p is co-supported by an encoded row at a distinct center of that cap
unimplemented_fail_closed: Euclidean coordinates and all metric equality, inequality, and full-radius-class disequality constraints
strict convexity, signed-area orientation, and the geometric StrictCapOrder theorem beyond the fixed cyclic order
arbitrary equal-distance pairs at a center that are not witnessed by one of the encoded four-point rows
the full U1LargeCapRouteBTailLiveData fields, U3FixedTriplePacket fields, and localized no-q-free fields outside the encoded CriticalShellSystem blocker consequence
the exact geometric no-M44 predicate beyond its card-profile consequence
all named-label placements or orbit representatives within either profile

verdict_policy.note: No result from this manifest is a Euclidean counterexample or a proof of the producer.
